text.title: claude-md-creation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for claude-md-creation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Too much information: documenting every edge case, every API endpoint, full architecture diagrams in prose.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overwhelming context budget. Put detailed architecture docs in .agents/ and reference them from CLAUDE.md with a single line.

failure-mode.description: Stale content: commands that no longer work, directory paths that no longer exist, env vars with wrong names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stale instructions mislead more than they help. An agent following a stale build command wastes tool calls diagnosing the failure rather than doing productive work.

failure-mode.description: Duplicate docs: same information already in README.md reproduced in CLAUDE.md.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates a maintenance burden and inconsistency. CLAUDE.md should contain only what Claude needs that differs from what a human developer would look up in README.

failure-mode.description: No structure: wall of text paragraphs without sections or headings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents scan CLAUDE.md for specific sections. Unstructured prose requires parsing the entire file to find a specific command or convention.

failure-mode.description: Wrong audience: written for human developers with background explanation and narrative prose.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CLAUDE.md is read by an agent, not a human onboarding. Omit explanations of why a technology was chosen; include only what the agent needs to operate correctly.
